name: mago-vscode-extension
description: PHP linting, formatting, and analysis via the Mago language server

mago.executablePath.description: Path to the mago binary built with language-server support. When empty, the extension first looks for vendor/bin/mago-lsp installed by the clearlyip/mago-lsp Composer package, then falls back to 'mago' on PATH.

mago.configPath.description:
Path to mago.toml configuration file (--config). If empty, mago will look for mago.toml in the workspace root.
This is the path to your `mago.toml` configuration file. If not specified, Mago will search for a `mago.toml` file in the workspace directory. If no configuration file is found, Mago will use default settings.

mago.workspace.description: Path to the workspace directory (--workspace). Overrides the VS Code workspace root passed to the mago language server.

mago.phpVersion.description:
PHP version to use for parsing and analysis, e.g. '8.3' (--php-version).
This should be a valid PHP version number (e.g., 8.0, 8.1). This value overrides the `php-version` setting in the configuration file and the `MAGO_PHP_VERSION` environment variable.

mago.threads.description:
Number of threads to use for linting, formatting, and analysis (--threads).
If not specified, Mago will use all available logical CPUs. This value overrides the `threads` setting in the configuration file and the `MAGO_THREADS` environment variable.

mago.allowUnsupportedPhpVersion.description:
Allow using an unsupported PHP version (--allow-unsupported-php-version).
Use this flag to bypass the check for supported PHP versions. This is not recommended, as it may lead to unexpected behavior.

mago.noVersionCheck.description:
Silence the project version drift warning (--no-version-check).
 Suppresses the warning emitted when the installed mago binary differs from the `version` pinned in `mago.toml`, as long as the drift is within the same major version. A major-version mismatch is *always* fatal and is not affected by this flag; the whole point of a major pin is to stop runs across incompatible config schemas.
Can also be set via the `MAGO_NO_VERSION_CHECK` environment variable or `no-version-check = true` in `mago.toml`.

mago.noAnalyzer.description:
Disable the static analyzer subsystem. 
With the analyzer off, capabilities that depend on its artifacts degrade: hover renders only the symbol kind (no type signature), completion drops `$obj->`, `$obj?->`, and `Class::` member lookups, and signature help, inlay hints, and code lenses go quiet. Goto definition for class-likes, free functions, and constants still works (those resolve from prelude metadata).

mago.noLinter.description:
Disable the linter subsystem (no lint diagnostics). 
No lint diagnostics are published, and `quickfix` code actions disappear since they're sourced from linter rules. If the analyzer is also off, the editor receives no diagnostics at all.

mago.noFormatter.description:
Disable the formatter subsystem ('Format Document' will be unavailable).
`textDocument/formatting` is dropped from the advertised server capabilities, so editors won't offer "format document" for files served by mago.

mago.maxRestartCount.description: Maximum number of times the language server will be automatically restarted on failure.

mago.hideStatusBarWhenIdle.description: Hide the Mago status bar item when the server is idle.

mago.trace.server.description: Traces the communication between VSCode and the Mago language server.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: Since this runs Psalm, and Psalm can be configured to execute code on your computer, you should avoid opening untrusted projects while using this plugin

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run check && npm run package
